D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

Fechner's Flicker Colours

slide the flicker rate and watch a black-and-white field bloom into faint colour

When a field switches black to white and back, the three cone channels lag by different amounts. Their mismatch in time is read by the brain as a tinge of colour.

Slide the flicker frequency and stare at the centre patch. Around five to ten flickers a second it stops looking purely grey and drifts toward a faint colour that the screen is not actually emitting.
